perturbation.original_text: Each party will comply with all law and regulations applicable such party's performance under this Agreement, including but not limited to U.S. Export Administration laws and regulations and any other export, import and re-export control laws applicable to such party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party will endeavor to comply with all laws and regulations applicable to such party's performance under this Agreement, including but not limited to U.S. Export Administration laws and regulations and any other export, import and re-export control laws applicable to such party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will comply' to 'will endeavor to comply' introduces ambiguity and weakens the obligation. Endeavoring to comply does not guarantee actual compliance, creating a loophole. This is especially problematic with export control laws, which demand strict adherence.
perturbation.law_explanation: The EAR requires strict compliance with export control regulations. By stating that a party will 'endeavor to comply,' the modified text fails to meet the legally mandated standard of full compliance, potentially leading to violations of export control laws.

perturbation.justification: The modification from 'will comply' to 'will endeavor to comply' weakens the legal obligation to adhere to export control laws. The Law Explanation states that the EAR requires strict compliance. The modified text fails to meet the legally mandated standard of full compliance, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: DIALOG will pay ENERGOUS the Royalties set forth in Exhibit B. For clarity, DIALOG will be responsible for paying to ENERGOUS any Royalties payable hereunder as a result of its Affiliates' Licensed Product Sales.
perturbation.changed_text: DIALOG intends to pay ENERGOUS the Royalties set forth in Exhibit B. For clarity, DIALOG will consider being responsible for paying to ENERGOUS any Royalties payable hereunder as a result of its Affiliates' Licensed Product Sales.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that DIALOG 'will pay' royalties, making it a firm obligation. The modified text introduces phrases like 'intends to pay' and 'will consider being responsible,' which create uncertainty and weaken the commitment. This makes the payment of royalties discretionary rather than mandatory.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Civil Code § 1657 states that 'If no time is specified for the performance of an act required to be performed, a reasonable time is allowed.' The original 'will pay' establishes a clear obligation, while 'intends to pay' makes the commitment vague. Under contract law, a promise with uncertain terms may be unenforceable, conflicting with the intention to create a legally binding agreement. The phrase 'will consider being responsible' also adds an unnecessary condition to the commitment.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CIV 1657. skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Civil Code - CIV DIVISION 3. OBLIGATIONS [1427 - 3273.69] ( Heading of Division 3 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 14. ) PART 2. CONTRACTS [1549 - 1701] ( Part 2 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 3. INTERPRETATION OF CONTRACTS [1635 - 1663] ( Title 3 enacted 1872. ) 1657. If no time is specified for the performance of an act required to be performed, a reasonable time is allowed. If the act is in its nature capable of being done instantly—as, for example, if it consists in the payment of money only—it must be performed immediately upon the thing to be done being exactly ascertained. (Enacted 1872.)
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the obligation to pay royalties from 'will pay' to 'intends to pay' and changes 'will be responsible' to 'will consider being responsible,' introducing ambiguity. This contradicts the legal requirement for clear contractual terms, as highlighted by the Law Explanation which states that 'a promise with uncertain terms may be unenforceable'. Scraped Law Snippet 1 (California Civil Code § 1657) states that if an act is capable of being done instantly and involves payment of money, it must be performed immediately, which the original text aligns with but the changed text undermines. The change in language creates an ambiguous legal obligation, making the contract potentially unenforceable under state law, thus creating a legal contradiction as defined by the prompt.

perturbation.original_text: ENERGOUS will keep the Deposit Materials in escrow and ensure on a quarterly basis that all the information relating to the Deposit Materials in escrow is current, including deposit of any Product Updates.
perturbation.changed_text: ENERGOUS should endeavor to keep the Deposit Materials in escrow and aim to ensure, when feasible, that the information relating to the Deposit Materials in escrow is reasonably current, considering the deposit of Product Updates.
perturbation.explanation: The original text contains a definite obligation for ENERGOUS to maintain current deposit materials in escrow. The modified text uses 'should endeavor,' 'aim to ensure,' and 'reasonably current,' weakening the obligation and introducing subjective criteria ('when feasible'). This weakens the requirement that ENERGOUS maintain up-to-date deposit materials.
perturbation.law_explanation: Under CUTSA, reasonable efforts must be made to protect trade secrets. By weakening the obligation to maintain current deposit materials, the modified text makes it more difficult for DIALOG to access and protect trade secrets related to the Products in the event of certain circumstances (such as an Insolvency Event of ENERGOUS). The escrow arrangement is designed to protect against misappropriation by ensuring DIALOG has access to necessary information. Diluting the requirement to keep materials current undermines this protection, potentially violating the spirit, if not the letter, of CUTSA regarding reasonable protective measures.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CIV 3426 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Civil Code - CIV DIVISION 4. GENERAL PROVISIONS [3274 - 9566] ( Heading of Division 4 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 16. ) PART 1. RELIEF [3274 - 3428] ( Part 1 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 5. UNIFORM TRADE SECRETS ACT [3426 - 3426.11] ( Title 5 added by Stats. 1984, Ch. 1724, Sec. 1. ) 3426. This title may be cited as the Uniform Trade Secrets Act. (Added by Stats. 1984, Ch. 1724, Sec. 1.)
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces ambiguities that weaken the obligation to maintain current deposit materials, potentially undermining the protection of trade secrets. The original text contains a definite obligation, while the modified text uses language like 'should endeavor,' 'aim to ensure,' and 'reasonably current,' which weakens the obligation and introduces subjective criteria. This contradicts the principle that 'reasonable efforts must be made to protect trade secrets' as ENERGOUS is expected to act. The snippet, CIV 3426 (Uniform Trade Secrets Act), establishes the legal framework for trade secret protection, and weakening the obligation could expose DIALOG to misappropriation.
